full_text: Hi guys, my name is Nitesh and you are welcome to my YouTube channel. In this video also we will continue our Lang chain playlist. In the last video we started reading rag and we focused on the theory around rag. I told you what is rag and why it is needed. I compared rag with fine tuning technique and today's video is continuation of the last video. We will make practically a rag based system using the Lang chain. The plan is that I will pick up a problem statement and create a rag based system around that problem statement. And we will do all this code in the Lang chain. Till now whatever you have read in the last 4-5 videos, document loaders, text splitters, vector stores, we will use all these in today's video and we will make a rag based system using them. So in the whole it's going to be a very interesting video. Let's start. So guys, first of all let's talk about problem statement. What are we going to make in this video? So we will work on a simple yet very important problem statement. And the name of our problem statement is YouTube chat. I don't know, maybe YouTube chat could be a good name. So basically what we are going to do is that we are going to make a rag based system with the help of which you can chat with any YouTube video in real time. So we all watch videos on YouTube. Some videos are very long, especially if you are watching a podcast, then they are 2-3 hours long. The problem is that if you want to understand the whole content of that video, then you will have to watch the whole video. Our solution solves this problem for you. For example, suppose I am watching a podcast which is 3 hours long. Suppose I get a question about that podcast, that is, is AI being discussed in this podcast? So I will immediately put a question in our system. And my rag based system will immediately reply to me that yes, AI has been discussed in this particular video and these are the key pointers. I can ask this quickly that can you summarize this whole video, in 5 bullet points, then my rag based system will quickly make a summary of the whole video and give it to me. If you are watching a lecture tomorrow on data science and you suddenly have some doubt in some part, then you will immediately put that doubt in this system and this system will solve that doubt and give it to you. So basically this is a chat system with the help of which you can ask about any YouTube video whatever you want to ask. So I am pretty sure you all can relate to what problem this system is solving. And that is why I thought this could be a very good problem statement for us. This system can be made in many ways. I mean, the final product can look like this. One option is that we make a Chrome plugin where the user will install this Chrome plugin. And when the video is running, it will click here and a chat interface will open and the user can chat here while watching the video. So this is the best solution if you can make it. But for this you will need the knowledge of HTML, CSS and JavaScript. The second option is if you do not know HTML, CSS and JavaScript, then what you can do is you can make a streamlit website where you will paste the link of the YouTube video and as soon as you click on submit, a chat window will open in a new window and you can chat about that video. So for this you should not need HTML, CSS and JavaScript, but you should be able to work with a tool like Streamlit. But since we are going to focus on rag, I will not focus on UI, but on functionality. And that is why whatever we will make, we will make it in a Google Colab notebook. But I would recommend that when you run this project in Google Colab, then definitely you should try and build a UI around this project. This will make your project look better and more usable. So in nutshell, this is the problem statement that we are targeting and we are going to build a rag system that will solve this problem statement. Next I will discuss the exact plan of action that how we will solve this problem statement. Now the interesting thing is that to make this project, we will use exactly the same flow that we discussed in the last video. If you remember, then I made this diagram in the last video and step by step I explained this diagram how a rag architecture works. Today's problem statement we will solve it on the basis of this flow, this architecture. So I will tell you step by step how our entire flow of building this project will be. So first of all you will load the transcript of your YouTube video that you want to chat with. So transcript is a file where whatever is said in the YouTube video is recorded in a place sentence by sentence. So our first goal is to fetch this transcript and bring it to our project. Now this work can be done in many ways. One way is that you can load a document loader in the lang chain which we call YT loader. Second option is that there are some APIs of YouTube that you can directly hit and load the transcript of any video. In our case we will use the APIs of YouTube. The reason for this is that I was trying to use the YouTube loader of the lang chain but it is a little buggy. In some videos it was working properly, in some videos the code was getting broken. That is why I am going to use this YouTube API. So in step 1 we will give the video's ID and load the transcript of that video. Now the transcript of the video will be quite big depending on the video. Then what we will do is we will apply a text splitter and divide that transcript into multiple chunks. Once we have multiple chunks then we will generate the embeddings of all the chunks and store them in a vector store. So in this step our whole indexing work will be completed. Which we discussed that step 1 is in any rag architecture. After that what we will do is we will create a retriever. For now we will use a simple similarity search based retriever. And we will send a query to that retriever. Now this retriever will do its work, embed this query and perform a semantic search in the vector store. And will bring you relevant documents. So this is step 2 retrieval. Once you have the relevant chunks and you have your query then you will merge these two and create a prompt. This will be step 3 which we call as argumentation. And once I have this prompt then I will send it to my LLM. And my LLM will understand the query, this context and will give me a response by flipping it. And this will be our fourth and final step that is generation. So I will show you all this step by step. And when this whole thing will start working then I will convert this whole thing in a chain. So that automatically the output of one component will be made of another component. And this whole chain will be executed automatically with the call of one invoke function.
